meta.name: YEVIB Phase 3 Intelligence Regression Matrix
meta.purpose: Tests whether YEVIB can qualify business signal, diagnose readiness, choose a useful strategy, and avoid overclaiming when source evidence is weak.

sites.label: Sydney CBD Plumbing
sites.notes: Local service test with clear service category, location signal, and emergency/service language.

sites.label: Lukins Plumbing
sites.notes: Local service test with Sydney-area signal, plumbing offer clarity, and service proof.

sites.label: Network Plumbing
sites.notes: Local service test with broad service signals, review/proof language, and strong local positioning.

sites.label: Go-To Skincare
sites.notes: E-commerce brand test with clear product positioning, founder/brand voice, and product truth.

sites.label: Sand & Sky
sites.notes: E-commerce brand test with ingredient claims, product values, and visible brand positioning.

sites.label: Allkinds
sites.notes: E-commerce brand test with product category clarity and audience positioning.

sites.label: Ozi Construction Sydney
sites.notes: Mixed-signal test: service and location exist, but page may be location-template style.

sites.label: Utopia Constructions
sites.notes: Mixed-signal test with some proof and history, but likely less content depth than stronger sites.

sites.label: Premier Construction
sites.notes: Mixed-signal test with service clarity, but may need caution if proof/source depth is thin.

sites.label: NicheWorks
sites.notes: Thin-signal test because the website is under construction and should trigger cautious output.

sites.label: Service Sites
sites.notes: Lower-content service website test. Should not be over-read if evidence is basic.

sites.label: LocalPages
sites.notes: Simple small-business website provider test. Useful for checking if YEVIB overclaims from generic service copy.
